title: Task 1: DB migration — add source_inbox_item_id and cancel_reason to Task
context:
Prerequisite for all other tasks in this initiative. See full design at docs/inbox-remediation-tracking-design.md.

=== app/models.py ===
In the `Task` class, add two new columns AFTER the existing `last_retry_reason` field:

  # Inbox linkage
  source_inbox_item_id = Column(String, ForeignKey('inbox_items.id', ondelete='SET NULL'), nullable=True)

  # Cancellation accountability
  cancel_reason = Column(String, nullable=True)

Note: Use ondelete='SET NULL' on the FK so deleting an inbox item doesn't cascade-delete the task.

=== app/schemas.py ===
Find the Task schema classes (TaskBase, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate, and whichever is the read schema). Add to each:
  source_inbox_item_id: Optional[str] = None
  cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None

Also create a new schema for the stuck remediations endpoint (used in Task 4):
  class StuckRemediation(BaseModel):
      task_id: str
      task_title: str
      inbox_item_id: str
      inbox_title: str
      reason: str  # 'stale_queued' | 'stale_in_progress' | 'silent_cancel'
      days_stale: float
      model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

=== Alembic Migration ===
Create a new migration following the pattern in docs/database-migrations.md:
  alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add_task_inbox_linkage_and_cancel_reason'

Verify the generated migration adds:
  op.add_column('tasks', sa.Column('source_inbox_item_id', sa.String(), nullable=True))
  op.add_column('tasks', sa.Column('cancel_reason', sa.String(), nullable=True))
  op.create_foreign_key(None, 'tasks', 'inbox_items', ['source_inbox_item_id'], ['id'], ondelete='SET NULL')

Run `alembic upgrade head` to verify it applies cleanly.
acceptance: 1) `Task` model has `source_inbox_item_id` (String, nullable, FK→inbox_items.id with SET NULL) and `cancel_reason` (String, nullable). 2) Alembic migration runs cleanly on existing DB. 3) Task schemas serialize both fields. 4) `StuckRemediation` schema defined in schemas.py. 5) `python -m pytest -v` still passes (no regressions).

title: Task 2: Set source_inbox_item_id when InboxProcessor creates tasks
context:
One-liner change in app/orchestrator/inbox_processor.py. Depends on Task 1.

In the `_create_task()` method of `InboxProcessor`, find where the `Task` object is constructed. After setting all current fields, add:

  task.source_inbox_item_id = inbox_item.id

The method signature is:
  async def _create_task(self, task_spec: dict[str, Any], inbox_item: InboxItem) -> Task | None:

The `inbox_item` parameter is the `InboxItem` model instance — `.id` is the inbox item's UUID string.

No other changes needed in this file.
acceptance: 1) `_create_task()` sets `task.source_inbox_item_id = inbox_item.id` before adding the task to the session. 2) Manual or automated test: trigger inbox processing on a needs_response thread → created task has `source_inbox_item_id` set to the inbox item's ID. 3) No other behavior changed.

title: Task 3: Enforce cancel_reason when rejecting tasks
context:
Update the task status update endpoint to require a reason code when setting status to 'rejected'. Depends on Task 1.

=== app/schemas.py ===
Find `TaskStatusUpdate` (or create it if it doesn't exist as its own schema). Update it to:
  class TaskStatusUpdate(BaseModel):
      status: str
      cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None

Add a constant for valid cancel reason codes (put it near the schema or in a shared constants file):
  VALID_CANCEL_REASONS = [
      'duplicate',
      'superseded',
      'out_of_scope',
      'blocked_indefinitely',
      'user_decision',
      'automated_cleanup',
  ]

=== app/routers/tasks.py ===
Find `PATCH /tasks/{task_id}/status` (or similar endpoint that handles status changes). Add validation:

  if status_update.status == 'rejected':
      if not status_update.cancel_reason:
          raise HTTPException(
              status_code=422,
              detail={
                  'error': 'cancel_reason required when rejecting a task',
                  'valid_reasons': VALID_CANCEL_REASONS
              }
          )
      task.cancel_reason = status_update.cancel_reason

Do NOT block other status transitions (completed, active, etc.) — only enforce on 'rejected'.

NOTE: If the code uses `task.status = status_update.status` directly, the cancel_reason assignment should come right after it within the same conditional block.
acceptance: 1) `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with `{status: 'rejected'}` (no cancel_reason) returns 422 with `valid_reasons` list in body. 2) `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with `{status: 'rejected', cancel_reason: 'user_decision'}` returns 200 and task.cancel_reason is set. 3) Other status transitions (completed, active) are unaffected — no cancel_reason needed. 4) Tests: write unit tests for all three cases in tests/test_tasks.py or equivalent.

title: Task 4: GET /api/inbox/stuck-remediations endpoint
context:
Add a new endpoint to the inbox router. Depends on Task 1. Full design at docs/inbox-remediation-tracking-design.md §2.4.

=== app/routers/inbox.py ===
Add after the existing endpoints (or before the delete endpoint for logical grouping):

  @router.get('/stuck-remediations')
  async def get_stuck_remediations(
      stale_queued_hours: int = 72,
      stale_in_progress_hours: int = 24,
      db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
  ) -> list[StuckRemediation]:
      """Return inbox-sourced tasks that appear stuck or silently cancelled."""

Imports needed:
  from app.schemas import StuckRemediation
  from app.models import Task as TaskModel, InboxItem as InboxItemModel
  from datetime import timedelta

Query logic (three separate queries then combine, or UNION — your choice, keep it simple):

  now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  stale_queued_cutoff = now - timedelta(hours=stale_queued_hours)
  stale_in_progress_cutoff = now - timedelta(hours=stale_in_progress_hours)

  # Approved but never started
  q1 = select(TaskModel, InboxItemModel).join(
      InboxItemModel, TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id == InboxItemModel.id
  ).where(
      TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id.isnot(None),
      TaskModel.status == 'active',
      TaskModel.work_state == 'not_started',
      TaskModel.updated_at < stale_queued_cutoff
  )

  # Started but gone quiet
  q2 = select(TaskModel, InboxItemModel).join(
      InboxItemModel, TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id == InboxItemModel.id
  ).where(
      TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id.isnot(None),
      TaskModel.status == 'active',
      TaskModel.work_state == 'in_progress',
      TaskModel.updated_at < stale_in_progress_cutoff
  )

  # Silently cancelled (rejected with no reason)
  q3 = select(TaskModel, InboxItemModel).join(
      InboxItemModel, TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id == InboxItemModel.id
  ).where(
      TaskModel.source_inbox_item_id.isnot(None),
      TaskModel.status == 'rejected',
      TaskModel.cancel_reason.is_(None)
  )

For each result row, build a StuckRemediation:
  - reason = 'stale_queued' | 'stale_in_progress' | 'silent_cancel'
  - days_stale = (now - task.updated_at).total_seconds() / 86400
  - Round days_stale to 1 decimal

Return combined list. No deduplication needed (a task can't match more than one query).
acceptance: 1) `GET /api/inbox/stuck-remediations` returns 200 with `list[StuckRemediation]`. 2) Auth required (401 without token). 3) Stale queued: tasks with source_inbox_item_id, status=active, work_state=not_started, updated_at > 72h ago appear. 4) Silent cancel: tasks with source_inbox_item_id, status=rejected, cancel_reason=None appear. 5) Healthy tasks do NOT appear. 6) Empty list `[]` on clean system. 7) Write a test in tests/ seeding 3 tasks (1 stale_queued, 1 silent_cancel, 1 healthy) and asserting 2 results.

title: Task 5: Wire stuck remediations into Daily Ops Brief
context:
Add a StuckRemediationsAdapter to BriefService so the daily brief surfaces stuck inbox-approved work. Depends on Task 4 AND the Daily Ops Brief feature (initiative: daily-ops-brief). Block this task until both are complete.

=== app/services/brief_service.py ===
Add a new adapter class alongside the existing adapters:

  class StuckRemediationsAdapter:
      """Returns a BriefSection listing inbox-sourced tasks that are stuck."""

      async def fetch(self, db: AsyncSession) -> BriefSection:
          try:
              # Reuse the same query logic as GET /api/inbox/stuck-remediations
              # Either import the helper function or duplicate the 3 queries here.
              # Recommended: extract the query into a shared function in app/services/remediation_queries.py
              # and import from there — avoids duplication between the endpoint and brief.
              items = await _fetch_stuck_remediations(db)  # shared helper

              brief_items = [
                  BriefItem(
                      source='remediations',
                      title=f"{item.task_title} [{item.reason}]",
                      detail=f"inbox: {item.inbox_title} — {item.days_stale:.1f}d stale",
                      priority='high' if item.days_stale > 5 else 'normal',
                  )
                  for item in items
              ]

              return BriefSection(
                  name='Stuck Remediations',
                  icon='⚠️',
                  items=brief_items,
                  available=True,
              )
          except Exception as e:
              return BriefSection(name='Stuck Remediations', icon='⚠️', items=[], error=str(e), available=False)

In BriefService.generate():
  - Add StuckRemediationsAdapter to the asyncio.gather() call
  - Only include the section in the returned DailyBrief if len(section.items) > 0
  - If 0 items: omit entirely (don't add it to sections list) — zero noise on clean days

=== Shared query helper ===
Create app/services/remediation_queries.py with:
  async def fetch_stuck_remediations(db, stale_queued_hours=72, stale_in_progress_hours=24) -> list[StuckRemediation]:
      # The 3 queries from Task 4, extracted here

Update app/routers/inbox.py to import and call this shared helper instead of inline queries.
acceptance: 1) Brief includes '⚠️ Stuck Remediations' section when stuck items exist. 2) Section is ABSENT when no items (zero noise guarantee). 3) Stuck items priority='high' when days_stale > 5. 4) Brief still renders even if StuckRemediationsAdapter throws (graceful degradation). 5) app/services/remediation_queries.py created and imported by both inbox router and brief service (no duplication). 6) Test: mock fetch_stuck_remediations to return 2 items → assert BriefSection present with 2 items. Mock to return [] → assert section absent.
